title: 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S-Class
summary: Review, Pricing, and Specs
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz is unveiling the 2027 Maybach S-Class, the automaker's ultra-luxury flagship sedan that combines cutting-edge technology with handcrafted elegance. The vehicle represents the latest iteration of Mercedes' most exclusive and expensive passenger car offering.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automotive manufacturer, is behind this release. The Maybach brand, originally a separate German automaker acquired by Daimler (Mercedes' parent company), has been revived as Mercedes' ultra-premium sub-brand since 2015.
whyMatters: The 2027 Maybach S-Class signals how legacy luxury automakers are competing in an increasingly electric and technology-driven market while maintaining exclusivity and heritage. This refresh matters to high-net-worth consumers and investors watching whether traditional luxury can adapt to changing buyer preferences around sustainability and autonomous features.
moreContext: The Maybach brand was dormant for decades before Mercedes relaunched it in 2015 to compete with Rolls-Royce and Bentley in the ultra-luxury segment. The current generation Maybach S-Class debuted in 2021 as an extended-wheelbase variant of the S-Class with bespoke interiors and personalization options. Mercedes has been incrementally updating this platform with new powertrains, infotainment systems, and design elements. The 2027 refresh comes as luxury automakers face pressure to electrify their lineups while maintaining the hand-assembled craftsmanship and exclusivity that justify $150,000+ price tags.

title: Audi Sport Quattro Makes 500+ Horsepower Return in Restomod Form
summary: Sure, it costs about as much as a nice house, but it looks properly mean and claims a better power-to-weight ratio than the Ferrari SF90.
whatHappening: Audi Sport is reviving the legendary Quattro with a high-performance restomod that produces over 500 horsepower. The modernized classic delivers a power-to-weight ratio that surpasses Ferrari's SF90 supercar.
whoInvolved: Audi Sport, the performance division of German automaker Audi, is behind this project. The company has a storied history of rally racing heritage and performance engineering, dating back to the original Quattro's dominance in the 1980s.
whyMatters: This restomod signals automakers' growing interest in reimagining classic models with modern performance technology as combustion engines face regulatory pressure. It appeals to enthusiasts willing to pay premium prices for exclusive, heritage-based vehicles with contemporary capabilities.
moreContext: The original Audi Sport Quattro debuted in 1980 as a rally legend, revolutionizing motorsport with its all-wheel-drive system. This restomod continues Audi's tradition of blending historic design with cutting-edge engineering. The project reflects broader industry trends where manufacturers create limited-edition restomods to celebrate brand heritage while commanding luxury price points comparable to supercars. The vehicle's positioning against the Ferrari SF90—a $500,000+ plug-in hybrid—underscores how restomod economics compete directly with new exotic cars.

title: Trump's Latest Pardons Include Diesel Tuners Who Violated Clean Air Act
summary: Nine people convicted of bypassing emissions controls or selling equipment to do so have had their convictions overturned.
whatHappening: Trump has pardoned nine people convicted of selling or installing diesel engine tuning devices that bypass emissions controls. The convictions have been overturned, freeing individuals prosecuted under clean air regulations.
whoInvolved: The pardoned individuals were convicted under federal environmental laws for modifying diesel engines, primarily pickup trucks, to remove pollution-control systems. Trump's pardon authority allows him to overturn convictions without requiring congressional approval.
whyMatters: The pardons signal a rollback of EPA enforcement priorities and weaken deterrents against emissions violations in the diesel aftermarket industry. This could embolden similar modifications and undermine decades of clean air standards while potentially facing legal challenges from environmental groups.
moreContext: Diesel tuning has been a persistent enforcement issue, with the EPA and DOJ prosecuting sellers of defeat devices—equipment designed to circumvent emissions standards—since at least the early 2000s. The practice increased visibility after the 2015 Volkswagen dieselgate scandal exposed how manufacturers used software to cheat emissions tests. These tuning modifications increase nitrogen oxide emissions and particulate matter, contributing to respiratory illness and smog. Trump's pardon aligns with his administration's pattern of reducing environmental regulations and reflecting skepticism toward climate and air quality enforcement.

title: 2026 Fiat Topolino
summary: What We Know So Far
whatHappening: Fiat is reviving the iconic Topolino nameplate for 2026 with a new electric vehicle. The compact car will target budget-conscious European buyers seeking affordable EV options.
whoInvolved: Fiat, the Italian automaker owned by Stellantis, is leading the revival. The company has a history of producing affordable, practical vehicles dating back to the original Topolino (1936-1955), which became a symbol of accessible transportation in post-war Europe.
whyMatters: The new Topolino represents Fiat's strategy to democratize electric vehicles by offering an affordable entry point into EV ownership for mass-market consumers. This move is critical as European automakers face pressure to transition away from combustion engines while maintaining accessibility across income levels.
moreContext: The original Topolino was one of Italy's most beloved cars and helped motorize the nation in the mid-20th century. Stellantis has been aggressive in launching affordable EVs across its portfolio to compete with Chinese manufacturers and Tesla in the budget segment. Fiat previously launched the 500 electric as a premium-priced option, but the Topolino revival signals the brand is doubling down on its heritage of practical, cost-effective vehicles for everyday drivers.

title: 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580 Drive: More, More, More
summary: How do you like it? If you want top-tier luxury with dashes of Mercedes flavoring, the V-8 Maybach delivers it all in ample doses.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Maybach has unveiled the 2027 S580, a ultra-luxury sedan packed with premium features and a powerful V-8 engine. The vehicle represents the automaker's latest push into the high-end luxury segment with abundant comfort and performance.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is behind the Maybach line, which targets ultra-wealthy consumers seeking the pinnacle of automotive luxury. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, tested and reviewed the vehicle.
whyMatters: The 2027 S580 signals Mercedes' continued investment in the ultra-luxury market despite economic uncertainty and shifting consumer preferences toward electric vehicles. Success in this segment demonstrates demand remains strong among affluent buyers willing to pay premium prices for exclusivity and traditional luxury experiences.
moreContext: The Maybach nameplate, originally a luxury brand from the 1920s-30s, was revived by Mercedes in 2002 as an ultra-premium sub-brand positioned above the S-Class. The V-8 engine choice reflects Mercedes' strategy to maintain gas powertrains in its highest-tier offerings, even as the broader automotive industry transitions to electrification. Maybach vehicles typically start at $180,000+ and cater to a niche market of executives, celebrities, and international elites seeking bespoke luxury experiences.

title: View Exterior Photos of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580
summary: See the exterior of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580 from every angle.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz has revealed exterior photos of the upcoming 2027 Maybach S580, showcasing the luxury sedan from multiple angles. Car and Driver is providing a comprehensive visual walkthrough of the vehicle's design and styling details.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is behind the 2027 Maybach S580. The Maybach sub-brand represents Mercedes' ultra-premium vehicle line, competing against Rolls-Royce and Bentley in the pinnacle luxury segment.
whyMatters: The 2027 Maybach S580 reveals Mercedes' design direction for its most exclusive sedan offering and signals the company's commitment to the ultra-luxury market. This preview helps potential buyers and industry observers understand what the next generation of Mercedes' flagship luxury vehicle will deliver in terms of styling and presence.
moreContext: The Maybach nameplate, revived by Mercedes in 2021, has become increasingly important to the brand's luxury portfolio alongside its S-Class flagship. The S580 designation reflects the powertrain upgrade from the previous generation, positioning it as the range-topper. Luxury automakers regularly use exterior reveals and design previews to build anticipation ahead of official launches. The 2027 model year redesign represents a significant refresh cycle for this segment, where styling, engineering, and technology updates are closely watched by affluent consumers and automotive enthusiasts.

title: View Interior Photos of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580
summary: See the interior of the 2027 Mercedes-Maybach S580 from every angle.
whatHappening: Mercedes-Benz is unveiling interior photos of its 2027 Maybach S580, offering a first look at the luxury sedan's cabin design and features from multiple angles. The imagery provides consumers with a comprehensive view of what to expect from the brand's ultra-premium flagship model.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-Benz, the German luxury automaker, is showcasing its Maybach line, which represents the pinnacle of the company's luxury vehicle offerings. The S580 is the newest iteration of this exclusive sedan segment.
whyMatters: These interior reveals signal Mercedes-Benz's commitment to competing in the ultra-luxury market against rivals like Rolls-Royce and Bentley. The 2027 model year updates help potential buyers and enthusiasts understand the latest innovations in high-end automotive interiors, influencing purchasing decisions in a segment where design and craftsmanship are paramount selling points.
moreContext: The Maybach nameplate represents Mercedes' most exclusive division, historically catering to ultra-wealthy consumers and executives. The S580 designation continues Mercedes' naming convention for its flagship sedan line. Luxury automakers typically release interior photography ahead of formal reveals to generate anticipation and media coverage among affluent demographics. The 2027 model year suggests this represents a mid-cycle refresh or generational update, a common strategy for maintaining competitive positioning in the luxury segment where subtle design evolution commands significant attention.

title: Cadillac Vistiqs Recalled for Possibly Dangerous Power-Folding Third Row
summary: Following Hyundai's second- and third-row recall earlier this year, GM tested the fold function on the Vistiq and found a similar issue.
whatHappening: General Motors is recalling Cadillac Vistiqs due to a potentially dangerous malfunction in the power-folding third-row seat mechanism. GM discovered the issue during testing and found it mirrors a similar problem that prompted Hyundai to recall vehicles earlier this year.
whoInvolved: General Motors and its luxury brand Cadillac are issuing the recall for the Vistiq, their three-row electric crossover. Hyundai previously identified comparable power-folding seat defects in second- and third-row configurations of its own vehicles.
whyMatters: The faulty power-folding mechanism poses a safety risk to passengers, potentially causing unexpected seat collapse or entrapment. This recall adds to growing concerns about power seat reliability across the auto industry and could impact consumer confidence in premium electric vehicle features.
moreContext: Power-folding seats have become popular convenience features in modern SUVs and crossovers, particularly in premium segments where the Vistiq competes. Hyundai's recall earlier this year flagged similar issues with automated third-row folding systems, prompting other manufacturers like GM to audit their own designs. The Vistiq, launched as Cadillac's answer to three-row electric crossovers, relies heavily on electronic features and automation as selling points. This recall highlights the emerging quality challenges automakers face as they rapidly introduce complex power mechanisms in new electric vehicle platforms.

title: The Only Cizeta Moroder V16T Supercar Ever Made Can Now Be Yours
summary: This crazy 16-cylinder prototype spawned a few production cars, but here's the only one to have music legend Giorgio Moroder's name on the back.
whatHappening: A rare Cizeta Moroder V16T supercar—the only one ever made with music legend Giorgio Moroder's name on it—is being offered for sale. This exotic prototype features a massive 16-cylinder engine and represents a unique piece of automotive and music history.
whoInvolved: Giorgio Moroder, the legendary music producer and composer known for his work on Flashdance and Scarface soundtracks, lent his name to this supercar collaboration. Cizeta, an Italian supercar manufacturer, created the vehicle as a limited-production hypercar project.
whyMatters: This car represents a rare intersection of music, design, and automotive engineering that appeals to collectors of both supercar memorabilia and music history. Its sale could set a precedent for celebrity-branded exotic vehicles and highlights the continued demand for one-off prototype supercars in the luxury market.
moreContext: The Cizeta Moroder V16T was conceived in the 1980s as an ambitious supercar project that combined Italian craftsmanship with cutting-edge engineering. While the prototype spawned a limited number of production vehicles, this particular example is distinguished by Moroder's personal involvement and branding. The 16-cylinder engine was a bold engineering choice that set it apart from competitors of the era. This sale represents a rare opportunity to own a piece of automotive history that bridges the worlds of high-performance cars and music culture.

title: Sumo-Sized: 1997 SUVs Compared
summary: Once again, we're faced with the age-old question: Is bigger necessarily better?
whatHappening: Car and Driver is comparing 1997 SUV models to evaluate whether larger vehicles deliver better performance and value. The publication revisits the fundamental question of whether size translates to superiority in the SUV market.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, is conducting the analysis. The piece examines various 1997 SUV manufacturers competing in the growing segment during the mid-1990s boom.
whyMatters: This comparison helps consumers navigate a rapidly expanding SUV market by clarifying which vehicles justify their size and premium pricing. The findings influence purchasing decisions during a period when SUVs are becoming mainstream alternatives to sedans and station wagons.
moreContext: The 1990s saw explosive growth in the SUV category as consumers prioritized perceived safety, cargo space, and commanding driving positions over fuel efficiency. Major manufacturers including Ford, Chevrolet, Dodge, and Toyota were aggressively expanding their SUV lineups to capture market share. This era marked the transition of SUVs from utilitarian work vehicles to family transportation, fundamentally reshaping the automotive industry. Car and Driver's comparative testing methodology helps establish benchmarks for evaluating vehicles across reliability, performance, and practicality metrics.

title: Elana Scherr: Restoring a 1970 Dodge Challenger, Again
summary: Second chances aren’t just for rom-coms. Sometimes project cars give you another shot at love—and paint.
whatHappening: Elana Scherr is undertaking a restoration project on a 1970 Dodge Challenger for the second time. The automotive enthusiast is getting another opportunity to bring the classic muscle car back to life, this time with a fresh approach to the work.
whoInvolved: Elana Scherr is a automotive journalist and TV personality known for her work covering cars and restoration projects. She's recognized in the automotive media space for her expertise and passion for classic vehicles.
whyMatters: The story highlights how passion projects can offer redemption and second chances, resonating with enthusiasts who've abandoned or restarted restoration work. It underscores the enduring appeal of 1970s American muscle cars and the personal connections owners develop with their vehicles.
moreContext: The 1970 Dodge Challenger represents the golden age of American muscle cars, a period when Detroit manufacturers competed fiercely on performance and style. Scherr's decision to restore the same model twice suggests either unfinished business from a previous attempt or a renewed commitment to perfecting the project. Classic car restoration has become increasingly popular among automotive enthusiasts and media personalities as a way to preserve automotive history. The Challenger specifically has experienced a resurgence in cultural relevance, appearing prominently in films and becoming a collector's favorite among muscle car aficionados.

title: 2000 Mitsubishi Pajero Mini on Bring a Trailer Is Perfect for a Peanuts Fan
summary: And we thought Snoopy only drove a Sopwith Camel.
whatHappening: A 2000 Mitsubishi Pajero Mini is listed for sale on Bring a Trailer, a popular online auction platform for classic and specialty vehicles. The listing has caught attention from Peanuts comic strip fans due to the vehicle's quirky charm and nostalgic appeal.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer is an online marketplace specializing in collector cars, classic vehicles, and automotive rarities. The Mitsubishi Pajero Mini is a compact SUV produced by Japanese automaker Mitsubishi, popular in Japan and select international markets.
whyMatters: This listing appeals to a niche but passionate audience of Peanuts enthusiasts and Japanese vehicle collectors, potentially driving up bidding interest beyond typical compact SUV buyers. The intersection of pop culture fandom and automotive collecting demonstrates how nostalgic appeal can drive collector car market demand.
moreContext: The Peanuts reference stems from the famous comic strip character Snoopy, whose fictional adventures often featured him imagining himself as a World War I flying ace piloting a Sopwith Camel. The Pajero Mini, produced from 1994 to 2012, became a cult favorite among compact SUV enthusiasts, especially in Japan where it remained popular for decades. Bring a Trailer has become known for highlighting quirky and culturally significant vehicles that attract bidders beyond traditional car collectors. The platform's auction format often results in strong prices for vehicles with compelling backstories or niche appeal.

title: The Toyota 4Runner's Defining Feature Isn't Four-Wheel Drive
summary: The 4Runner's lore is built on off-road adventure, sure. But it's also about that power window in the liftgate.
whatHappening: Car and Driver argues that the Toyota 4Runner's cultural identity extends far beyond its legendary off-road capabilities. The vehicle's defining characteristic is actually the power window in its liftgate—a seemingly minor feature that has become iconic to the SUV's legacy.
whoInvolved: Toyota manufactures the 4Runner, one of the longest-running SUV nameplates in automotive history. Car and Driver is a leading automotive publication that analyzes vehicles through both performance and cultural lenses.
whyMatters: This perspective reframes how consumers and enthusiasts think about vehicle identity and brand heritage. It highlights how seemingly small design details can become more memorable and meaningful than headline specifications, influencing purchasing decisions and brand loyalty.
moreContext: The 4Runner has been in production since 1984 and built a reputation as a rugged, adventure-capable SUV that could handle extreme terrain and conditions. Over decades, the power liftgate window became a practical feature that owners valued for convenience during off-road trips and daily use. The vehicle's cultural cachet derives not just from capability specs but from the accumulated small details and lived experiences of its owners, making it emblematic of how automotive legacy is constructed through both engineering and mythology.

title: Ford's $30,000 Electric Truck Comes into Focus in New Spy Photos
summary: We're still wondering what the future pickup-like EV will be called, but the name Ranchero feels like a real possibility.
whatHappening: Ford's upcoming affordable electric truck is taking shape, with new spy photos revealing design details of the highly anticipated $30,000 EV. The automaker hasn't officially confirmed the vehicle's name, though internal speculation suggests it could be called the Ranchero, reviving a classic nameplate.
whoInvolved: Ford is developing this electric pickup as part of its broader EV strategy to compete in the mass-market segment. The company has been aggressively investing in electric vehicles to challenge Tesla's dominance and meet changing consumer demand.
whyMatters: A $30,000 electric truck could democratize EV ownership by making electric pickups accessible to mainstream buyers, a critical market segment in the U.S. This move positions Ford to capture price-conscious consumers who've been priced out of premium EV offerings, potentially reshaping the affordable truck market.
moreContext: Ford has committed billions to EV development, already launching the Mustang Mach-E and F-150 Lightning. The auto industry is racing to offer affordable electric vehicles as battery costs decline and competition intensifies. The Ranchero name—originally used for a car-truck hybrid produced from 1959 to 1979—would signal Ford's intention to create a unique vehicle category rather than a direct F-150 rival. Pricing this model at $30,000 would undercut most current EV competitors and address the industry's push toward making electrification economically viable for average buyers.

title: Drivers' Parade Returns to F1 Grid with Smaller, Faster Lego Race Cars
summary: The new Lego F1 minicars are smaller than last year and made with fewer bricks, but their top speed is now a blazing 15 mph.
whatHappening: Formula 1's iconic drivers' parade is returning to the grid, but this time featuring miniature Lego race cars instead of full-size vehicles. The new Lego F1 cars are smaller and more efficient than last year's version, constructed with fewer bricks while achieving an impressive top speed of 15 mph.
whoInvolved: Formula 1 and Lego have partnered to create this novelty attraction for the sport's pre-race festivities. Both companies are global leaders in their respective fields—F1 is motorsport's premier racing series, while Lego is the world's largest toy manufacturer by revenue.
whyMatters: This innovation brings a playful, accessible element to F1's traditional pageantry, potentially engaging younger audiences and families who might not otherwise connect with the sport. The engineering achievement of making faster, more efficient Lego vehicles demonstrates continued innovation in the partnership and could inspire interest in both racing and construction toys.
moreContext: The drivers' parade is a long-standing F1 tradition where competitors wave to fans before each race. Last year's iteration featured Lego F1 minicars, establishing this year's redesign as part of an ongoing collaboration. The new vehicles represent an engineering improvement despite using fewer bricks, suggesting Lego designers optimized weight distribution and aerodynamics. This continues the trend of F1 embracing entertainment elements alongside competitive racing to broaden its appeal.

title: This 1975 Chevy Caprice Classic Convertible on Bring a Trailer Got a Late Start in Life
summary: First titled in 2021, it has 951 miles.
whatHappening: A 1975 Chevy Caprice Classic Convertible is being auctioned on Bring a Trailer with an unusually low mileage of 951 miles. The vehicle was first titled in 2021, meaning it sat unused for roughly 46 years before entering the market.
whoInvolved: Bring a Trailer is an online auction platform specializing in collector and classic vehicles. The platform connects sellers of rare or desirable cars with enthusiast buyers worldwide.
whyMatters: Ultra-low-mileage classic cars command premium prices among collectors seeking pristine examples. This Caprice's 46-year storage period and minimal use make it a rare find for buyers seeking original condition vehicles from the muscle car era.
moreContext: The 1975 Caprice Classic was one of Chevrolet's final full-size convertibles before the company discontinued the body style, making surviving examples increasingly collectible. Second-generation Caprices (1971-1976) are prized for their size, V8 performance, and American muscle car aesthetic. Bring a Trailer has become a bellwether for collector car values, with auction results often setting market precedents. Low-mileage classics from the 1970s have seen growing collector interest as original muscle car inventory diminishes.

title: View Photos of the 2027 Ferrari 12Cilindri Manuale
summary: See the Ferrari 12Cilindri Manuale inside and out from every angle.
whatHappening: Ferrari is unveiling the 2027 12Cilindri Manuale, a new high-performance supercar featuring a manual transmission. The automaker is showcasing the vehicle through detailed photography from multiple angles.
whoInvolved: Ferrari, the Italian luxury sports car manufacturer, is behind this release. The company has long been synonymous with high-performance vehicles and is one of the few remaining automakers offering manual transmissions in flagship models.
whyMatters: The manual transmission option appeals to driving enthusiasts at a time when most manufacturers are phasing out stick shifts for automatics and electric drivetrains. This move signals Ferrari's commitment to preserving the traditional driving experience in an increasingly automated industry.
moreContext: Ferrari's 12-cylinder engines represent the pinnacle of the brand's engineering, delivering exceptional power and performance. The resurgence of manual transmissions in limited-production supercars reflects a niche but passionate market segment of purists who value mechanical engagement and control. Car and Driver's photo gallery provides comprehensive visual documentation of the vehicle's design, interior, and styling details. This announcement comes as the automotive industry continues its shift toward electrification, making manual supercars increasingly rare and collectible.

title: Which Version of the Ford Explorer Is the Best Deal for the Money?
summary: The sporty-looking Explorer offers exceptional value if you are shopping for a three-row family SUV. We'll help you narrow down the trim levels to the best choice for you.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is evaluating Ford Explorer trim levels to help consumers identify the best value option in the three-row SUV market. The publication highlights that the Explorer delivers strong value proposition across its lineup despite competitive market pressures.
whoInvolved: Ford Motor Company manufactures the Explorer, one of the most established three-row SUV nameplates in the American market. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, provides expert analysis and buying guidance to consumers navigating vehicle purchases.
whyMatters: Three-row SUVs represent a major purchase category for families, making trim-level guidance critical for budget-conscious buyers seeking maximum value. With rising vehicle prices across the industry, understanding which configurations offer the best return on investment directly impacts household purchasing power and satisfaction with long-term ownership.
moreContext: The Explorer has evolved from its truck-based roots into a car-like crossover platform, modernizing its appeal while maintaining spacious three-row seating. The current generation competes against rivals like the Chevrolet Traverse and Honda Pilot in a segment that has seen increased pricing pressure. Automakers have expanded trim offerings significantly, creating complexity that leaves buyers uncertain about which packages justify their costs. Publications like Car and Driver address this by comparing feature sets, performance, and pricing across trim levels to simplify decision-making for practical shoppers.

title: Ferrari Revives the Gated Shifter with the New 12Cilindri Manuale
summary: The 12Cilindri Manuale feels like it has a traditional manual transmission, but there's no physical connection—it's a simulation.
whatHappening: Ferrari is introducing a simulated manual transmission in its new 12Cilindri Manuale, featuring a traditional gated shifter that creates the tactile experience of a manual without any physical mechanical connection to the engine. The car delivers the mechanical feel and engagement drivers crave while using modern automatic transmission technology underneath.
whoInvolved: Ferrari, the iconic Italian luxury automaker known for high-performance sports cars, is behind this innovation. The company has a storied history of manual transmissions but has largely shifted to automatics in recent decades due to performance and efficiency demands.
whyMatters: This move signals Ferrari's attempt to preserve the emotional connection drivers have with manual transmissions while acknowledging that modern performance and emissions standards make traditional manuals impractical. For enthusiasts, it offers the last chance to experience gated shifter engagement in a new Ferrari, potentially becoming a highly collectible future classic.
moreContext: Manual transmissions have nearly disappeared from high-performance cars as dual-clutch and automatic transmissions outperform them in acceleration and fuel efficiency. Ferrari phased out traditional manuals years ago, but enthusiasts have consistently lamented their loss. This simulated manual approach—similar to video game force-feedback controllers—allows Ferrari to offer nostalgia and engagement without sacrificing the 12Cilindri's modern performance capabilities. The gated shifter, a mechanical component that guides gear selection through defined positions, remains an iconic element of classic Ferrari DNA.

title: View Photos of the 2026 Volkswagen Atlas SEL Premium R-Line
summary: See the interior and exterior of the 2026 Volkswagen Atlas SEL Premium R-Line from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is releasing an exclusive photo gallery showcasing the 2026 Volkswagen Atlas SEL Premium R-Line. The images provide a comprehensive 360-degree view of both the interior and exterior of the updated mid-size SUV.
whoInvolved: Volkswagen, the German automaker, is introducing its refreshed Atlas lineup for the 2026 model year. Car and Driver, a leading automotive journalism publication, is providing detailed visual coverage of the vehicle.
whyMatters: This visual showcase helps prospective buyers evaluate the Atlas's design, features, and styling before the vehicle hits dealerships. The timing of these photos provides early insight into Volkswagen's three-row SUV strategy in a highly competitive segment where design and interior quality significantly influence purchasing decisions.
moreContext: The Volkswagen Atlas is a mid-size, three-row SUV that competes against vehicles like the Honda Pilot, Toyota Highlander, and Kia Sorento. The R-Line trim typically features sportier styling, enhanced suspension, and premium interior appointments compared to base models. The 2026 model year refresh suggests updates to exterior design elements, infotainment systems, and potentially powertrain options as Volkswagen continues its electric vehicle transition while maintaining combustion engine options for traditional buyers.

title: Audi Says Markets Are Too Different for a 'Global Car' to Work
summary: Audi and AUDI, its Chinese spinoff brand, are heading in different directions to remain competitive across contrasting market trends.
whatHappening: Audi is abandoning the concept of a single global car platform, instead tailoring vehicles separately for different regional markets. The German automaker and its Chinese spinoff brand AUDI are diverging strategies to stay competitive in their distinct automotive landscapes.
whoInvolved: Audi, the German luxury car manufacturer owned by Volkswagen Group, is splitting focus between its core European and global operations and AUDI, a standalone Chinese brand designed specifically for that market's unique demands and consumer preferences.
whyMatters: This shift signals a major strategic pivot away from the cost-saving model of global platforms that automakers have pursued for decades. The move reflects how fragmented today's auto markets have become, with China, Europe, and North America each requiring fundamentally different vehicle designs and features to succeed.
moreContext: Automakers have traditionally sought to maximize profitability through platform sharing across markets, reducing development costs. However, Chinese consumers increasingly demand locally-tailored features, EV capabilities, and technology integration that differ significantly from Western preferences. China's rapid EV adoption, strong domestic competition, and different regulatory standards have forced global automakers to reconsider the one-size-fits-all approach. Audi's decision follows similar moves by other luxury brands recognizing that regional market divergence now outweighs the economies of scale in global platforms.

title: These Are the Biggest Winners and Losers in Auto Sales in Q2 2026
summary: Sedans seem to be making a comeback and Stellantis had a couple of big improvements, while some important Toyotas and Fords struggled.
whatHappening: Sedans are staging an unexpected comeback in Q2 2026 auto sales, bucking years of consumer preference for SUVs and trucks. Stellantis notched significant sales gains while Toyota and Ford faced headwinds with struggling key models.
whoInvolved: Stellantis, the multinational automotive manufacturer formed from the 2021 merger of Fiat Chrysler and PSA Group, is capitalizing on market shifts. Toyota and Ford, two of the world's largest automakers with deep roots in sedan manufacturing, are grappling with changing market dynamics.
whyMatters: The sedan resurgence could signal a shift in consumer priorities toward fuel efficiency and affordability as gas prices and economic pressures mount. For Stellantis, strong Q2 performance could reshape investor confidence and competitive positioning, while Toyota and Ford's struggles threaten market share in a critical segment they've historically dominated.
moreContext: The auto industry has spent the past decade pivoting aggressively toward SUVs and crossovers as consumer preferences shifted and profit margins improved on larger vehicles. However, rising interest rates, inflation concerns, and mixed economic signals in 2026 are prompting buyers to reconsider fuel-efficient sedans as practical alternatives. Stellantis has leveraged its diverse brand portfolio—including Jeep, Ram, and Peugeot—to capture market opportunities across segments, while traditional sedan leaders like Toyota's Camry and Ford's Fusion have faced increased competition from new entrants and overlooked product refreshes.

title: Right Now You Can Buy a Polestar 4 for Less Than a New Chevy Bolt
summary: Last week's U.S. sales ban spurred a fire sale on the remaining Polestar 3 and 4 inventory—and you won't believe the deals.
whatHappening: Polestar is slashing prices on remaining Polestar 3 and 4 inventory in the U.S., making them cheaper than a new Chevy Bolt. The fire sale was triggered by last week's U.S. sales ban on the vehicles.
whoInvolved: Polestar, the Swedish electric vehicle brand owned by Volvo and Geely, is conducting the clearance. Chevrolet's Bolt serves as the price comparison benchmark in the EV market.
whyMatters: These deals represent a rare opportunity for EV buyers to access premium Polestar models at significantly reduced prices. The clearance could accelerate Polestar's market exit or restructuring in the U.S., potentially affecting the brand's long-term presence and credibility with consumers.
moreContext: The U.S. sales ban on Polestar vehicles represents a significant regulatory or trade action that forced the company to liquidate its remaining North American inventory quickly. Polestar had been positioning itself as a premium EV alternative to Tesla and traditional luxury brands. The Chevy Bolt has become a benchmark for affordable EV pricing, making it a notable comparison point. This fire sale highlights the vulnerability of smaller EV manufacturers to sudden policy changes and market disruptions in the competitive electric vehicle landscape.

title: Trump Refuses to Renew USMCA: Here's What That Means for Autos
summary: The trade agreement still has 10 years left, but the president's refusal to extend the deal puts the automotive sector in jeopardy.
whatHappening: President Trump is refusing to renew the USMCA (United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement), the trade deal governing North American commerce. This decision creates uncertainty for the automotive sector despite the agreement having 10 years remaining on its current term.
whoInvolved: Trump and his administration are making the trade policy decision. The automotive industry—including major manufacturers and suppliers operating across the U.S., Mexico, and Canada—faces direct consequences from any changes to the trade framework that governs their operations.
whyMatters: The automotive sector relies heavily on cross-border supply chains under USMCA's favorable terms, and uncertainty about renewal could disrupt manufacturing, increase costs, and trigger investment pullbacks. A collapse or renegotiation of the deal could threaten jobs and competitiveness across North America in the short term, with long-term implications for vehicle pricing and availability for consumers.
moreContext: The USMCA replaced NAFTA in 2020 and includes provisions specifically designed for automotive trade, such as rules of origin requirements and labor standards. The automotive industry has already faced supply chain pressures from pandemic disruptions and the semiconductor shortage, making additional trade uncertainty particularly precarious. Mexico has become a critical manufacturing hub for U.S. automakers, producing millions of vehicles annually and serving as a major source of auto parts. Trump's refusal to commit to renewal signals potential renegotiation, tariffs, or more restrictive trade policies that could reshape how North American auto production operates.

title: TuxMat’s Custom-Fit Floor Mats Are Engineered to Elevate Your Interior
summary: Precision fit, three-layer construction, and the kind of detail most floor mats overlook.
whatHappening: TuxMat is promoting its custom-fit floor mats as a premium interior upgrade, emphasizing precision engineering and multi-layer construction. The product targets car owners seeking higher-quality alternatives to standard factory or generic aftermarket mats.
whoInvolved: TuxMat, an automotive accessories manufacturer, is the focus of this feature in Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication with a large enthusiast audience. The company specializes in precision-fit interior products designed for specific vehicle models.
whyMatters: Quality floor mats protect vehicle interiors from wear, moisture, and dirt while improving aesthetics—factors that affect resale value and driving experience. As consumers increasingly invest in vehicle customization and long-term maintenance, premium mat options represent a growing segment of the aftermarket accessories market.
moreContext: The aftermarket floor mat category has traditionally been dominated by basic, one-size-fits-all products or expensive OEM options. Custom-fit mats have gained traction as 3D manufacturing and precision tooling have made vehicle-specific products more affordable and accessible. Car and Driver's coverage suggests TuxMat's three-layer construction and attention to detail differentiate it in a competitive market where fitment and durability are primary customer concerns.

title: Every EV from Every Brand in America
summary: We've created this handy guide to let you know all the brands that sell electric vehicles in the United States.
whatHappening: Car and Driver has published a comprehensive guide cataloging every electric vehicle available from all brands currently selling in the United States. The resource provides consumers with a complete snapshot of the EV market landscape as it exists right now.
whoInvolved: Car and Driver, a major automotive publication, compiled this guide. The guide covers offerings from established automakers (Tesla, Ford, GM, BMW, etc.) and newer EV-focused brands (Rivian, Lucid, Polestar) all competing in the U.S. market.
whyMatters: This guide helps consumers navigate an increasingly crowded EV market with clearer options and comparisons. As automakers rapidly expand their electric lineups and new entrants launch vehicles, having a centralized reference point matters for purchasing decisions and understanding market penetration across price points and vehicle types.
moreContext: The U.S. EV market has accelerated dramatically over the past 3-4 years, with major manufacturers committing billions to electrification and dozens of new models launching annually. Federal incentives like the $7,500 tax credit have spurred consumer interest, while stricter emissions regulations push automakers to expand their EV portfolios. The competitive landscape now includes legacy automakers alongside new startups, creating a fragmented market where consumers struggle to track available options. This guide addresses that fragmentation by aggregating vehicles across all brands and segments.

title: 2027 Cadillac Escalade IQ / IQL
summary: Review, Pricing, and Specs
whatHappening: Cadillac has unveiled the 2027 Escalade IQ and IQL, introducing a new generation of its flagship luxury SUV with updated styling, technology, and powertrain options. The models represent a significant refresh of General Motors' premium full-size SUV lineup.
whoInvolved: General Motors and its luxury brand Cadillac are behind the redesign. Cadillac has been repositioning itself as GM's ultra-premium brand to compete directly with Mercedes-Benz, BMW, and Range Rover in the high-end SUV market.
whyMatters: The new Escalade generation is critical for Cadillac's profitability and brand perception, as the SUV segment commands premium pricing and strong consumer demand. Success here will determine whether Cadillac can sustain its luxury positioning and compete effectively against foreign competitors in the lucrative full-size SUV category.
moreContext: The Escalade has been Cadillac's volume driver and status symbol since its 1999 debut, undergoing major redesigns roughly every decade. The previous generation launched in 2021, and this 2027 refresh continues GM's strategy of electrification and advanced technology integration across its luxury portfolio. Cadillac faces intensifying competition from Range Rover's full-size offerings and Mercedes' GLS models, particularly as luxury buyers increasingly demand cutting-edge infotainment systems and electrified powertrains. Pricing and available powertrains—particularly the IQ designation suggesting potential electrification—will be key differentiators in this highly competitive segment.

title: 2026 Mercedes-AMG SL63 S E Performance Test: Sound and Fury
summary: The SL63 S E Performance's potent plug-in hybrid powertrain generates some serious numbers, at a cost.
whatHappening: Mercedes-AMG has unveiled the 2026 SL63 S E Performance, a plug-in hybrid roadster that delivers impressive performance metrics through its advanced powertrain. Car and Driver has tested the vehicle and found it generates serious power numbers, though at a premium cost.
whoInvolved: Mercedes-AMG, the high-performance division of Mercedes-Benz, engineered this model as part of the luxury automaker's shift toward electrified performance vehicles. Car and Driver, a leading automotive publication, conducted the evaluation and assessment.
whyMatters: This represents Mercedes-AMG's strategy to maintain performance credentials while meeting increasingly stringent emissions regulations through hybridization. The model signals how luxury automakers are balancing raw power delivery with environmental compliance, a trend that will define the next generation of high-end vehicles.
moreContext: Mercedes-AMG has progressively electrified its lineup over recent years, adding plug-in hybrid options to flagship models to reduce emissions without sacrificing driving dynamics. The SL roadster has been a cornerstone of Mercedes' sports car portfolio for decades, and this latest generation reflects the industry-wide pivot toward hybrid and electric powertrains. Competitors like BMW and Porsche have similarly introduced high-powered plug-in hybrids in their premium segments. The SL63 S E Performance variant represents the top-tier offering in the refreshed SL lineup, combining traditional engine performance with electric motor assistance.

title: 2027 Lamborghini Urus SE Performante Ups the Ante on Hybrid Power
summary: The Performante name returns, now with an 801-hp plug-in hybrid powertrain, new air suspension, and copious carbon fiber.
whatHappening: Lamborghini is reviving its Performante nameplate for the 2027 Urus SE, equipping the luxury SUV with an 801-horsepower plug-in hybrid powertrain. The vehicle also features a redesigned air suspension system and extensive carbon fiber components throughout its construction.
whoInvolved: Lamborghini, the Italian supercar manufacturer owned by Audi (part of Volkswagen Group), is developing this next-generation performance SUV. The company has a history of using the Performante designation for its most track-focused, high-performance variants.
whyMatters: This move signals Lamborghini's commitment to electrification while maintaining extreme performance credentials in the competitive luxury SUV segment. The 801-hp hybrid setup positions the Urus SE Performante as a bridge between traditional combustion performance and future EV requirements, appealing to buyers demanding both power and environmental responsibility.
moreContext: The original Performante name earned legendary status through models like the Gallardo Performante and Huracán Performante, which pushed Lamborghini's vehicles to their dynamic limits. The Urus, launched in 2018, became Lamborghini's fastest-selling model, proving strong market demand for high-performance luxury SUVs. This 2027 refresh comes as automakers increasingly adopt plug-in hybrid technology to meet emissions regulations while maintaining range and performance. Carbon fiber has become standard on Lamborghini's ultra-premium offerings, reducing weight to maximize the hybrid system's efficiency gains.

title: View Interior Photos of the 2027 Lamborghini Urus SE Performante
summary: See the cabin of the 2027 Lamborghini Urus SE Performante from every angle.
whatHappening: Car and Driver is releasing interior photos of the upcoming 2027 Lamborghini Urus SE Performante, showcasing the cabin design from multiple angles. The images provide the first detailed look at the luxury SUV's interior styling and features.
whoInvolved: Lamborghini, the Italian luxury automaker owned by Audi, is developing the 2027 Urus SE Performante as part of its next-generation lineup. Car and Driver, a major automotive publication, is breaking the exclusive photos.
whyMatters: The interior reveals signal Lamborghini's design direction for its flagship SUV and what buyers can expect from the electrified performance variant. This gives potential customers and enthusiasts their first concrete look at the cabin before the vehicle's official debut.
moreContext: The Urus SE Performante represents Lamborghini's shift toward electrification while maintaining its performance heritage. The original Urus launched in 2018 as the brand's first SUV and became a commercial success. The SE variant indicates a shift toward hybrid or electric powertrains as the industry transitions away from traditional combustion engines. Performante models typically feature enhanced styling, upgraded interiors, and optimized performance compared to standard variants.
